title: Bridge Essay: Prediction and perception: Insights for (and from) tinnitus.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Dependent arising — predictive processing, free energy principle, active inference as causal cognition.

**Prediction and perception: Insights for (and from) tinnitus.**. Neurosci Biobehav Rev.. PMID: 30998951. DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2019.04.008. More than 150 years have passed since Helmholtz first described perception as aprocess of unconscious inference about the causes of sensations. His ideas have since inspired awealth of literature investigating the mechanisms underlying these inferences. In recent years, much of this work has converged on the notion that the brain is ahierarchical generative model of its environment that predicts sensations and updates itself based on prediction errors.
